summary: Backend-only testing for TiffinFlow. Built /app/backend/tests/test_tiffinflow_backend.py with 36 pytest cases covering auth (register/login/me/profile), menu (week/today), plans, subscriptions (wallet/cod/stripe + pause/resume), orders (wallet/cod/stripe), wallet (balance/txns/recharge validation), delivery (role-guard, OTP transitions), admin (stats/users/orders, RBAC) and webhook reachability. After fixing 3 backend bugs related to MongoDB _id leaking into responses, all 36 tests pass (100%).

minor.issue: Returns 500 when body has no 'type' field (KeyError inside emergentintegrations.handle_webhook). Outer try/except converts to HTTPException(400) only when CheckoutError is raised, but raw POSTs without signature still produce 'webhook parse failed' log noise. Endpoint behaves correctly (non-404), but should gracefully reject malformed payloads with 400 rather than relying on library exception.

action_items: Consider auditing all routes that return dicts mutated by Motor's insert_one — same _id leak pattern could recur if devs add new endpoints. A small helper like `await _insert(db.col, doc)` that pops _id post-insert (or always using projection-fetch after insert) would harden the codebase.
Webhook handler logs an exception traceback for every malformed payload — consider catching at a higher level and silently returning 400.

CORSMiddleware uses allow_origin_regex='.*' WITH allow_credentials=True. Browsers will accept this since the regex echoes Origin in ACAO, but it's effectively open. Lock down to known preview/prod origins.
critical_code_review_comments: MongoDB ObjectId leak (fixed): /api/auth/register, _activate_subscription (subscribe wallet/cod), and /api/orders/create (wallet/cod paths) all returned dicts mutated by insert_one which appended a non-serializable _id. login already popped _id but the rest did not. Fixed by adding `dict.pop('_id', None)` after each insert_one. Recommend a single helper.

Cookie auth always overrides Authorization header in get_current_user(). This is fine for browsers but trips up shared-session API clients (testing/SDKs). Consider preferring Authorization when both are present.
JWT_SECRET committed to /app/backend/.env. Move to a secret manager for prod.

Stripe payment fulfillment relies on polling /api/payments/status/{session_id} from the frontend. The webhook route exists but does nothing (returns received: True). This is acceptable but should be documented; otherwise a customer who closes the success page may never see their wallet credited.
No rate-limiting/brute-force protection on /api/auth/login — playbook recommends lockout after 5 failures. Not implemented.

Bcrypt hash format verified (starts with $2b$) ✓. httpOnly Secure SameSite=None cookies set ✓. Seed admin only inserts if missing — password rotation via env var won't update existing admin (playbook recommends updating). Currently OK because admin password unchanged.
/api/auth/me does not include wallet_balance freshness — fetched from cache at fixture time only matters for tests; route itself fetches fresh from DB ✓.

/api/subscriptions/pause 'extends=False' when target date is today after 10am IST — correct cutoff logic. However if user pauses a FUTURE date, extends is always True (correct). No tests exercise the after-10am-today path due to time dependency.

/api/delivery/assigned returns all preparing/out_for_delivery orders globally rather than orders assigned to the specific delivery partner. There is no per-partner assignment field. Acceptable for MVP, but flag for scaling.
/api/admin/stats revenue counts only payment_transactions.amount where payment_status='paid' — excludes wallet-paid subscriptions/orders and COD-collected revenue. May understate true revenue.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (added .pop('_id', None) in 3 places: register response, _activate_subscription, create_order wallet/cod paths)
test_credentials: Admin admin@tiffinflow.com/admin123, Delivery delivery@tiffinflow.com/delivery123, Customer created on-the-fly via /api/auth/register with test_user_<rand>@example.com
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates one fresh customer (test_user_*@example.com) with ₹100 welcome balance; persists subscriptions/orders in DB. No cleanup teardown — recommend periodic mongo cleanup of test_user_* docs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_tiffinflow_backend.py -v` for regression. Important: do NOT share a requests.Session across users — backend get_current_user prefers cookies over Authorization header, so cookies from one login will override subsequent Bearer tokens. Use plain requests.* or per-user sessions. Stripe Checkout calls hit a real sandbox via emergentintegrations; session_id and URL are returned but never navigated. Webhook test only verifies endpoint exists (not 404); full signature flow not testable without Stripe-signed payloads.
rca of the issue: All three POST 500s during initial run traced to a single root cause: Motor's `collection.insert_one(doc)` mutates the input dict by appending an `_id: ObjectId(...)` key. The handlers then returned that same dict to FastAPI, which couldn't JSON-serialize ObjectId. The login route already had `user.pop('_id', None)` but register/_activate_subscription/create_order(wallet|cod) did not. Reproduction: `curl -X POST /api/auth/register -d '{"email":"x@y.z","password":"p","full_name":"x"}'` → 500. Mitigation applied: pop _id post-insert in all 3 places. Long-term: wrap insert_one in a helper that always strips _id, or use `find_one({'id': ...}, {'_id':0})` to re-fetch after insert.
